programmes.show_name: Ella Fitzgerald: Just One Of Those Things
programmes.show_description: Documentary on the life of jazz singer Ella Fitzgerald, showcasing her impact on civil rights and her lasting legacy. From Emmy-nominated filmmaker Leslie Woodhead.

programmes.show_name: Luther Vandross: Live at Wembley Arena
programmes.show_description: Revisit Luther Vandross's 1988-89 world tour as the American singer-songwriter performed at Wembley Arena.
A look at the rise of The Beach Boys, the iconic rock/pop group who rose to stardom with their happy, fun-loving songs that were filled with beautiful harmonies. (S5, ep 9)

programmes.show_name: The Beach Boys - Pet Sounds: Classic Albums
programmes.show_description: Brian Wilson and other members of the Beach Boys give an insight into the making of their 1966 critically acclaimed album, Pet Sounds.
Sir Antonio Pappano inaugurates a new era for the London Symphony Orchestra. This opening night of the new season welcomes Norwegian violinist Vilde Frang.
Evening Waterfall: Bob shares a truly unique piece while painting a flowing river with rapids on black canvas. (S12, ep 8)
Tropical Seascape: Bob demonstrates how to paint an exotic ocean masterpiece, complete with palm trees swaying in the warm breeze. (S12, ep 9)
A Time To Die: Yves's marriage has gone stale and his young mistress has announced that she is pregnant. But he then makes a decision which changes everyone's lives forever. (S9, ep 8)
Mr Know-All: When pretty hotel worker Elly is charmed by a guest, archaeologist Max Kelada, it seems that the Mr Know-All professor might not be the man he claims to be. (S9, ep 9)
Safe Conduct: An American journalist meets a soccer star while traveling behind the Iron Curtain, but the authorities arrest him as a suspected smuggler. (S1, ep 21)
Place of Shadows: A young man tries to access a monastery in pursuit of revenge over a thief who he blames for ruining his life. (S1, ep 22)
A look at the life and career of the award-winning director behind movies such as The Manchurian Candidate, Birdman of Alcatraz, The Train and Ronin. (S3, ep 6)
A look at the life and career of screen icon Al Pacino, who shot to fame in mafia masterpiece The Godfather, before starring in Serpico, Scarface and Scent of a Woman. (S10, ep 1)
The Man From The South: A young American couple on holiday meet a mysterious old gentleman who makes them a macabre wager. (S1, ep 1)
Mrs Bixby and the Colonel's Coat: Unable to show her dentist husband Cyril the fur coat her lover has given her, Mrs Bixby proceeds to hatch a clever plan. (S1, ep 2)
Kate Bryan explores the founding of the National Gallery, talking to curators about the original collection and taking a close look at the building's architecture. (1 of 2)
The Falkirk Wheel: Stephen Mangan hosts the grand finale with just three artists who have made it through to the last round of the competition. (S11, ep 8)
Winner's Film: Join the winner of this year's Sky Arts Landscape Artist of the Year as they travel to County Mayo to research their £10,000 prize commission. (S11, ep 9)
A look at Bill Murray, famed for his BAFTA-winning performance in Lost in Translation, his collaborations with Wes Anderson and roles in Groundhog Day and Ghostbusters. (S10, ep 2)
William and Mary: When her husband William dies, Mary Pearl is shocked that his will stipulates that his brain should be kept alive - with interesting results. (S1, ep 3)
Lamb to the Slaughter: When Mary kills her husband with a frozen leg of lamb, she must think quickly to hide the evidence. (S1, ep 4)
Back for Christmas: Before leaving for America, a man kills his wife and buries her. Once Stateside, he receives messages telling him the murder will be exposed. (S1, ep 23)
The Perfect Murder: Worried that their Aunt Rosalie doesn't really like them, brothers Paul and Henri set out to get their hands on her fortune any way they can. (S1, ep 24)
Accompanied by the Johann Strauss Orchestra, world renowned violinist Andre Rieu performs numbers including Roses from the South, and Emperor's Waltz at Schonbrunn in Vienna.
Barry Cryer looks back on the lives and careers of the family comedy act, with their successful run on Broadway and in film spanning over 40 years. (S2, ep 1)
Film reflecting on the highs and lows of the Emmy-winning comedian and actor's life. Featuring in-depth interviews with Mike Epps, Tiffany Haddish and his widow, Jennifer Lee.
Extraordinary artists, designers and members of the community come together to dress some of Liverpool's most iconic statues for a unique summer project brought to you by Sky Arts.
Artists and local communities join forces to compete for the chance to create the UK's next major landmark in this exciting series hosted by Gemma Cairney. Series finale. (S1, ep 7)
At Bonhams, a collection of intimate letters written by a recently widowed Jackie Kennedy that she sent to British diplomat David Ormsby-Gore go under the hammer. (S8, ep 11)
There's a bidding frenzy in New York as the work of Jean-Michel Basquiat goes up for auction, with his painting 'Untitled' set to break records. (S8, ep 12)
In celebration of his 30-year career, the late Pavarotti is accompanied by the Philharmonia Orchestra as he performs O Sole Mio and Nessun Dorma in a concert in Hyde Park.
Mountain at Sunset: In the space of just half an hour, Bob breaks down how to create a brown-toned mountain scene on canvas. (S12, ep 10)
Soft Mountain Glow: In this masterpiece, Bob uses all of his skill and experience to paint a tranquil landscape setting nestled at the base of a towering mountain. (S12, ep 11)

A look at the life and career of the Academy Award-winning director behind The Sound of Music, West Side Story and Star Trek: The Motion Picture. (S3, ep 7)
A look at Bill Murray, famed for his BAFTA-winning performance in Lost in Translation, his collaborations with Wes Anderson and roles in Groundhog Day and Ghostbusters. (S10, ep 2)

programmes.show_name: Classic FM's Rising Stars With Julian Lloyd Webber
programmes.show_description: The world-renowned cellist and conductor showcases 30 of the best musicians under 30, from cellists and organists to composers and trumpeters.
Follow Finlay Macdonald, musical director of the National Piping Centre, plus a number of Scotland's leading pipers at Piping Live - the celebratory international festival. (1 of 3)
A look into the life and career of the American film director behind All the President's Men and Sophie's Choice, and the producer of To Kill a Mockingbird. (S3, ep 8)
From the Man with No Name to a two-time Academy Award-winning director and Hollywood legend, explore the life and career of actor, filmmaker and politician Clint Eastwood. (S10, ep 3)
The Landlady: When a young man arrives in Bath, he is shocked to find his seemingly charming landlady has a dark side. (S1, ep 5)
Neck: A young lady is cut down to size by her husband's loyal butler when it is discovered she is having an affair. (S1, ep 6)
There was an Old Woman: With only one thing in mind, the dishonest Frank and Lorna Bramwell visit the home of wealthy, eccentric Monica Laughton - intent on robbing her. (S1, ep 25)
Whodunit: Recently deceased, Alexander Penn Arlington gets permission from the recording angel to relive his last day on earth, so that he can discover who killed him. (S1, ep 26)
Kate journeys through the gallery's 200-year history with a look at the Turner bequest, before exploring the impressionist works by Van Gogh, Monet and Seurat. (2 of 2)

programmes.show_name: Liza Minnelli: The Last Heiress of Hollywood
programmes.show_description: This documentary is a portrait of an exceptional American talent, unforgettable in her role as Sally Bowles in Cabaret.
Glenn Close embodies neither Hollywood's beauty standards nor its sugar-coated perfection. The actress portrays women fighting for equality and their strength to rise again.

programmes.show_name: Paul McCartney And Wings: One Hand Clapping
programmes.show_description: Filmed at Abbey Road Studios in 1974 on videotape, experience the inner workings of the band as they perform together.
